name of your workflow. In the GitHub Actions tab, you will see ESLint CI.
name: ESLint CI 

on defines when this workflow should run.
Someone pushes code to main then Run Eslint CI
the workflow also runs when a Pull Request is targeting the main branch.
name: ESLint CI
"on":
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main
A workflow contains one or more jobs.
currently have one job: lint
GitHub -> Starts Ubuntu runner -> Runs the "lint" job there
A job contains multiple steps.
Step 1: Get your code
name is the label shown in the GitHub Actions UI.
uses means you're using a reusable GitHub Action.
action/checkout means -> checks out your Git repository onto the runner.
After this step:

Ubuntu Runner
│
├── package.json
├── package-lock.json
├── eslint.config.js
├── src/
└── ...

Now the runner has your project files.

Step 2: Set up Node.js
Step 3: Install dependencies
jobs:
  lint: 
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 24
          cache: npm
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
      - name: Run ESLint
        run: npm run lint
